seo.meta_description: Import a 2014 Jaguar F-Type from Japan to NZ with clear landed pricing, auction documents before bidding and local support through the process.
seo.h1_tag: Import a 2014 Jaguar F-Type to NZ
seo.og_title: Jaguar F-Type 2014 – import direct from Japan
seo.og_description: Bring in a 2014 Jaguar F-Type from Japan with transparent landed costs, auction paperwork before you bid and NZ-based support from enquiry to delivery.

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2014 Jaguar F-Type to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Bring in a 2014 Jaguar F-Type direct from Japan with clear costs, auction paperwork up front and NZ-based support from first enquiry to handover.
proof_points: Japan auction access for Jaguar sports models
Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: The 2014 Jaguar F-Type is for drivers who want a rear-wheel-drive sports car with sharp handling and a proper sense of occasion. It’s a two-seater that works for weekend trips, open-road drives and the odd commute when you feel like taking the long way home.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes — from Japan auction to NZ road-ready
savings_block.body: For a 2014 Jaguar F-Type, you start with the Japanese auction hammer price, then add shipping, marine insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our service fee to reach the landed cost. We lay out these items in writing before you bid so you can see how the total comes together. That way you’re choosing a car based on real numbers, not guesses.

cta_form.headline: Find your Jaguar F-Type 2014 — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Take a closer look at the 2014 F-Type’s low seating position, wide centre console and simple switchgear — this is the view you’ll have on your next Sunday run.
blocks.main_usp_deep: A 2014 Jaguar F-Type from Japan gives you classic front-engine, rear-wheel-drive balance and a choice of supercharged V6 or V8 performance, depending on what comes up at auction. Many cars are well optioned, with features like leather trim, premium audio and parking sensors common on Japanese-market examples. Importing lets you target lower mileage cars and specific colours or wheel packages that are harder to find locally. We focus on clean auction sheets and service records where available so you know what you’re bidding on.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You’re likely a driver who already has a practical daily and wants something special for weekends and clear-road mornings. You care more about steering feel and engine sound than boot space, and you’re comfortable with higher running costs in exchange for a proper sports car experience. For that kind of buyer, a 2014 F-Type is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’ve been helping New Zealanders bring in cars from Japan since 2009, including performance and sports models like the F-Type. Our role is to translate the auction process, check the documentation and give you clear landed pricing before you commit. You see the auction sheet, photos and our notes on condition and any modifications. A real NZ team stays in touch from first enquiry through to the car arriving and clearing entry checks.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives the F-Type the classic sports car feel on NZ back roads and open highways.

car_specs_benefits.spec: Supercharged V6 and V8 petrol options
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Lets you choose between a more usable daily performance level or a higher-output weekend toy, depending on what appears at auction.
Keeps weight down and makes the car feel focused, ideal if you already have a family car for other duties.

car_specs_benefits.spec: Automatic transmission with manual shift mode
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Easy in traffic, but you can still take control on twisty roads or track days.
Often lower mileage and better maintained than similar-age imports from other regions, which can help with long-term ownership.

cta_mid_page.headline: Start a search for your 2014 F-Type
cta_mid_page.subtext: Tell us the colour, engine and budget you have in mind and we’ll come back with real auction options.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how different 2014 Jaguar F-Type variants typically compare when you’re looking at Japanese auctions.

rows.notes: For a 2014 F-Type, you’re within the ESC and safety thresholds needed for NZ entry. Later facelift years exist, but this page focuses on 2014-only cars.
Expect supercharged V6 and V8 petrol engines with varying outputs. Higher-output trims usually cost more at auction and can attract higher running costs.
Higher trims may add larger wheels, upgraded brakes, sports exhaust, different seats and extra comfort features. We note these on each auction listing we send you.

Being a 2014 performance car, it typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no structural modifications are noted. Loud aftermarket exhausts or major suspension changes can trigger extra checks.
F-Types appear less often than mainstream models, so you may need to wait a little for the right colour, engine and mileage combination. Rarer trims can take longer and cost more.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For performance cars like the F-Type, we flag any modification notes on the auction sheet before shortlisting.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common models, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2014 F-Type is straightforward, but it helps to know where the main risks sit.

items.risk: Worrying about buying a high-performance car you haven’t seen in person.
items.solution: We base our advice on the full auction sheet, photos and any service records, and talk you through what they mean in plain language before you decide to bid.

items.risk: Concern about hidden costs on top of the auction price.
items.solution: We outline the expected auction price range, shipping, insurance, port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our fee so you can see the full landed estimate up front.

items.risk: Uncertainty around auction grades and what they say about condition.
items.solution: We explain the grade, note any repair history or panel work on the sheet and highlight areas that may need attention once the car arrives.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the year, budget and spec you’re aiming for, and we keep an eye on the Japanese auctions for matching 2014 F-Types. When good options appear, we send you the sheets, photos and landed cost estimates so you can choose whether to bid. If you’d like to explore it, send us a brief and we’ll talk you through the next steps.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2014 Jaguar F-Type from Japan?
faq_block.a: Total cost depends on the auction hammer price, mileage, trim and shipping at the time. On top of the auction price you’ll have shipping, marine insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our fee. We give you a written estimate before you commit to bidding.

faq_block.q: How long does it take for a 2014 F-Type to arrive in NZ?
faq_block.a: Once we win an auction, shipping to NZ usually takes around 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. Allow extra time for export paperwork in Japan, NZ entry checks and any compliance work needed before you can drive it.

faq_block.q: Will a 2014 Jaguar F-Type meet NZ entry standards?
faq_block.a: A 2014 F-Type is within the right age and safety window and usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and there are no major modifications. Final approval always depends on physical inspection and checks at NZ entry certification.

faq_block.q: What auction grade should I look for on a 2014 F-Type?
faq_block.a: Higher grades usually mean better overall condition, but the panel notes and diagrams matter just as much. We explain the grade, point out any repairs or paintwork and help you weigh up condition against price.

faq_block.q: Can you help with parts and servicing once the car is here?
faq_block.a: We don’t run a workshop, but we can point you toward NZ garages familiar with European performance cars. Most routine parts for a 2014 F-Type are available locally or can be sourced through specialist suppliers.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to explore a 2014 Jaguar F-Type import?
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us what you’re looking for and we’ll come back with real Japanese auction options, landed cost estimates and next steps.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on Jaguar F-Type listings in Japan, fresh auction examples and import tips for performance cars.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based team that’s been importing vehicles from Japan since 2009. We focus on clear landed pricing, full auction documentation and steady communication from first enquiry to delivery. Sports cars like the Jaguar F-Type sit alongside everyday imports in the cars we help bring in each year.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost covers everything from the winning bid in Japan through to the car being ready for registration in NZ.

rows.notes: The hammer price you pay for the 2014 F-Type at a Japanese auction, plus local auction fees.
Transport from Japan to NZ and marine insurance to cover the car while it’s on the water.
NZ port charges, entry certification, any compliance work required and associated inspection fees.

auction_checklist.check: Auction grade and repair history notes
auction_checklist.why: Shows whether the F-Type has had panel repairs, accident damage or structural work that could affect value and compliance.

auction_checklist.check: Underbody and corrosion photos where available
auction_checklist.why: Helps spot any underbody damage or corrosion that could lead to extra work at NZ entry certification.
Regular servicing is important on performance engines and can reduce the chance of expensive surprises later.
Worn tyres or kerbed wheels are common on sports cars and can add to your initial on-road costs.

auction_checklist.check: Aftermarket exhaust, suspension or wheel modifications
auction_checklist.why: Non-factory parts can affect noise levels, ride quality and compliance checks, so it’s important to know what’s fitted.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You need four or five seats and a big boot for family duties.
You’re looking for low running costs and modest insurance premiums.
You prefer a simple, low-powered car and don’t want to think about performance servicing or premium tyres.

alternatives_block.intro: If a 2014 F-Type isn’t quite right, there are other Japanese-import sports and GT options worth considering.

rows.better_if: You want a mid-engined feel and very precise handling.
rows.tradeoff: Cabin and styling feel different to the F-Type, and parts pricing can be higher in some areas.

rows.better_if: You’d like a folding hardtop and a more relaxed GT character.
rows.tradeoff: Less dramatic styling and a different engine character compared with the Jaguar.

rows.better_if: You want a simpler, naturally aspirated V6 sports car with strong aftermarket support.
rows.tradeoff: Interior and badge feel less premium than the Jaguar, but running costs can be easier to manage.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Engine and drivetrain overview
engine_drivetrain_block.body: The 2014 Jaguar F-Type line-up centres on supercharged V6 and V8 petrol engines driving the rear wheels through an automatic transmission. These engines deliver strong performance and a distinctive sound, but they do reward regular servicing and good quality fluids. When we review auction listings, we look for signs of careful ownership and note any modifications that could affect how the car drives or how it’s assessed at NZ entry.
checklist: Confirm which engine variant the car has and whether it matches the auction description.
Check for any mention of oil leaks, engine noise or warning lights on the auction sheet.
Look for notes about transmission behaviour, such as harsh shifts or slipping.
Review underbody photos for signs of impact damage around the drivetrain.
Note any aftermarket performance parts that could affect reliability or compliance.

fuel_use_block.headline: Fuel use and day-to-day running
fuel_use_block.body: A 2014 Jaguar F-Type is a performance-focused petrol sports car, so you should plan for higher fuel use than a small hatch or sedan. Short trips, spirited driving and city traffic will all push consumption up. Many owners treat the F-Type as a second car for weekends and special drives, which can make the running costs easier to live with.
items: Use premium petrol to keep the supercharged engine running as intended.
Budget for higher fuel use if you do mainly short or stop–start trips.
Factor in premium tyres and performance brake pads as part of your running costs.

service_history_block.headline: Service history and maintenance
service_history_block.body: Service history matters on a 2014 F-Type, especially given the performance on offer. At auction we look for stamped books, dealer or specialist receipts and any timing or cooling system work noted. Once the car arrives in NZ, it’s sensible to do a fresh service and inspection with a workshop that understands European performance cars.
items: Prioritise cars with documented regular servicing and mileage that matches the records.
Ask about common wear items like brakes, suspension bushes and cooling components.
Plan for slightly higher servicing costs than a mainstream hatch or sedan due to parts and labour.

mechanical_risks_block.headline: Mechanical risks to be aware of
mechanical_risks_block.body: Any 2014 performance car carries some mechanical risk, and the F-Type is no different. Higher mileage, hard use or poor servicing can lead to issues that aren’t always obvious in photos alone. Our job is to highlight what we can see in the documentation so you can decide whether the risk level suits you.
items: Performance engines and gearboxes can be expensive to repair if neglected, so service history is important.
Aftermarket tuning or track use can accelerate wear on brakes, suspension and driveline components.
Minor oil weeps or seepage may be noted on auction sheets; we’ll flag these so you can factor in potential remedial work.

model_deep_dive_block.headline: 2014 Jaguar F-Type: what to know before you import
model_deep_dive_block.body: The 2014 F-Type launched Jaguar back into the modern sports car space, with a focus on sound, styling and rear-drive handling. Japanese-market cars often come with desirable options such as upgraded wheels, premium audio and various interior trims. When we shortlist cars, we look at mileage, service records, options, colour and any signs of repair work so you can compare like for like. That way you’re choosing between specific cars, not just a model name.
items: Coupe and convertible body styles may both appear at auction, each with different practicality and roof mechanisms.
Wheel and brake packages vary, and larger setups can mean higher replacement costs later.
Interior trims range from more understated combinations to bright colours; we can help you target what you prefer.

trim_comparison_block.headline: Understanding 2014 F-Type trims at auction
trim_comparison_block.body: Japanese auction listings don’t always translate trim names cleanly, so it helps to know what to look for. Higher trims usually bring more power, different suspension tuning and extra comfort or tech features. We decode the listing details and photos so you can see what each car actually offers.
items: Base V6 models typically focus on balanced performance and comfort, often at a lower auction price.
V6 S and V8 variants add more power and may include sports exhausts, uprated brakes and different wheels.
Special editions or option packs can add unique colours, interior trims or extra equipment that affect desirability and price.
